perturbation.original_text: 1.2 “Deliverables” means the physical goods, to be supplied and/ or services rendered in terms of the Order ;and shall include any activity, work and/or services to be rendered in conjunction with the Delivery of goods and vice versa;
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 “Deliverables” means the physical goods to be supplied and/ or services rendered in terms of the Order; however, 'Outputs' may also refer to similar or different items or services, as determined by Grindrod SA.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of "however, 'Outputs' may also refer to similar or different items or services, as determined by Grindrod SA" introduces ambiguity. It is now unclear if obligations concerning 'Deliverables' also apply to 'Outputs', creating a potential contradiction in terms of fulfilling the Order, specially when referring to 'Delivery' in section 1.3, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.3 “Delivery” means the completion and/or Delivery of Deliverables in a condition and place acceptable to the Buyer, Acceptance of the Deliverables is not considered to have taken place until payment has been effected.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces the term 'Outputs' which may or may not be equivalent to 'Deliverables'. Section 1.3 defines 'Delivery' as the completion of 'Deliverables', and acceptance only occurs after payment. However, if 'Outputs' are different from 'Deliverables' as suggested by the change, it is unclear whether the 'Delivery' definition applies to 'Outputs', leading to a contradiction regarding the conditions for completion, acceptance, and payment related to 'Outputs' versus 'Deliverables'. This creates ambiguity and potential conflict as it is not clarified what constitutes delivery and acceptance for 'Outputs' if they are distinct from 'Deliverables'.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information excludes information which is already in the possession, or under the control of the Vendor otherwise than as a result of having been disclosed by Grindrod SA to the Vendor or as a result of the preparation and execution of a proposal or any other interaction with Grindrod SA.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information excludes information which is already in the possession, or under the control of the Vendor, or 'Contractor', even if obtained through Grindrod SA, unless explicitly marked confidential.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the original intent by implying that information obtained from Grindrod SA isn't confidential unless specifically marked, regardless of how the Vendor obtained it. This directly contradicts the undertaking in section 3 regarding the use and disclosure of confidential information, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. For the purposes of this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Undertaking, The Vendor undertakes; 3.1. to use such Confidential Information only for purposes of performing as required in terms of its obligations / promises or duties arising out of the Agreement / understanding /Request for Quotation / Contract or Purchase Order and for no other purpose whatsoever, 3.2. to hold any such Confidential Information in strict confidence and under proper and reasonable physical security, 3.3. not to disclose such Confidential Information to any person without the prior written authorization from Grindrod SA, 3.4. to, if the Vendor is in doubt concerning the confidentiality of any information or whether the Vendor is authorized to disclose it to any person, request and abide by any written ruling given by Grindrod SA, 3.5. to bring to the attention of Grindrod SA, in writing, any abuse or unauthorized disclosure of such Confidential Information of which the Vendor becomes aware, 3.6 that the provisions of this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Undertaking shall survive the termination or expiration of any Agreement / understanding / Request for Quotation / Contract / Purchase Order or any interaction, with Grindrod SA, of whatsoever nature.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Confidential Information' by excluding information obtained from Grindrod SA unless explicitly marked confidential. This directly contradicts section 3, which mandates the Vendor to treat all Confidential Information from Grindrod SA with strict confidence and not disclose it without prior written authorization. The changed text allows for the potential disclosure of information obtained from Grindrod SA if it's not explicitly marked confidential, thereby creating a clear conflict with the obligations outlined in section 3.

perturbation.original_text: 1.  The Vendor and Grindrod SA have entered into an agreement / understanding / request for quotation / contract / order or any interaction of whatsoever nature, under which the Vendor may be required to quote on and / or render Deliverables to Grindrod SA and/or Grindrod SA shall be obliged to disclose or otherwise make available to the Vendor, Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.  The Vendor or 'Affiliate' and Grindrod SA have entered into an agreement / understanding / request for quotation / contract / order or any interaction of whatsoever nature, under which the Vendor may be required to quote on and / or render Deliverables to Grindrod SA and/or Grindrod SA shall be obliged to disclose or otherwise make available to the Vendor, Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The introduction of 'Affiliate' without defining it creates ambiguity. It's unclear if the obligations of the 'Vendor' also apply to the 'Affiliate,' especially regarding confidential information. This contradicts the assumption that only the Vendor is bound by the confidentiality agreement, creating uncertainty regarding who is responsible for safeguarding sensitive data as reffered in the clauses of point 3, leading to in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. For the purposes of this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Undertaking, The Vendor undertakes; 3.1. to use such Confidential Information only for purposes of performing as required in terms of its obligations / promises or duties arising out of the Agreement / understanding /Request for Quotation / Contract or Purchase Order and for no other purpose whatsoever, 3.2. to hold any such Confidential Information in strict confidence and under proper and reasonable physical security, 3.3. not to disclose such Confidential Information to any person without the prior written authorization from Grindrod SA, 3.4. to, if the Vendor is in doubt concerning the confidentiality of any information or whether the Vendor is authorized to disclose it to any person, request and abide by any written ruling given by Grindrod SA, 3.5. to bring to the attention of Grindrod SA, in writing, any abuse or unauthorized disclosure of such Confidential Information of which the Vendor becomes aware, 3.6 that the provisions of this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Undertaking shall survive the termination or expiration of any Agreement / understanding / Request for Quotation / Contract / Purchase Order or any interaction, with Grindrod SA, of whatsoever nature.
perturbation.justification: The introduction of 'Affiliate' in the changed text without a clear definition creates ambiguity regarding who is bound by the confidentiality obligations outlined in Clause 3. The original text explicitly states 'The Vendor' undertakes the obligations, whereas the changed text makes it unclear if 'Affiliate' also shares these responsibilities. This directly contradicts the explicit obligations placed solely on 'The Vendor' in the contradicted text, especially concerning the handling and non-disclosure of confidential information, leading to a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1.5 “Purchase Order” means the purchase order placed for deliverables;
perturbation.changed_text: 1.5 “Purchase Order” means the purchase order placed for deliverables; however, 'Procurement Request' may also initiate the procurement process.
perturbation.explanation: Introducing 'Procurement Request' creates confusion. It's now unclear if the obligations associated with a 'Purchase Order' also apply to a 'Procurement Request'. This creates in-text contradiction with section 2 that refers to fulfilling obligations that came from the purchase order.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. The Parties are aware that Confidential Information may be disclosed or otherwise be made accessible to the Vendor for the purpose of the Vendor fulfilling its obligations / promises or duties arising from the Agreement / understanding / Request for Quotation / Contract or Purchase Order agreed to and accepted by the Vendor.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Purchase Order'. The changed text introduces 'Procurement Request' as potentially initiating the procurement process alongside 'Purchase Order'. The contradicted text refers to obligations arising from a 'Purchase Order'. The introduction of 'Procurement Request' creates ambiguity as to whether those same obligations apply, creating a strong in-text contradiction because it's unclear if obligations in section 2 apply to both or just 'Purchase Order'.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1. to use such Confidential Information only for purposes of performing as required in terms of its obligations / promises or duties arising out of the Agreement / understanding /Request for Quotation / Contract or Purchase Order and for no other purpose whatsoever,
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1. to use such Confidential Information only for purposes of performing as required in terms of its obligations / promises or duties arising out of the Agreement / understanding /Request for Quotation / Contract or Purchase Order or 'Project Scope' and for no other purpose whatsoever,
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'Project Scope' introduces another layer of ambiguity because it is not defined. It is not clear weather project scope means the same obligations, or new obligations with regards to 'Confidential Information' in section 3.3, generating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.3. not to disclose such Confidential Information to any person without the prior written authorization from Grindrod SA,
perturbation.justification: The addition of 'Project Scope' in section 3.1 introduces ambiguity because it is not defined. This ambiguity creates a contradiction with section 3.3, which requires prior written authorization for disclosing 'Confidential Information' to any person. The change makes it unclear whether disclosing information within the 'Project Scope' is subject to the same authorization requirement, creating a direct conflict with the existing clause and therefore creating a contradiction.
